subject: Databases
title: Consensus Protocols
description: A short lesson to help you apply Consensus Protocols.

hook.text: Three nodes must agree on which transaction to commit next. If two agree, it's committed. That's consensus.

buildup.text: Raft and Paxos are consensus algorithms. They guarantee agreement even if some nodes fail, given a majority survives.

discovery.text: Raft is simpler: a leader proposes, followers vote, majority wins. If the leader dies, a new election starts.

twist.text: Consensus has a cost: every write requires a round trip to a majority of nodes. Latency increases with cluster size.

climax.text: etcd (Raft), ZooKeeper (Zab), and CockroachDB (Raft) all use consensus. It's the foundation of distributed correctness.

punchline.text: Consensus: majority agreement. Correct even when some nodes fail.

quiz.question: Why does consensus add latency to writes?
options: Every write must wait for a majority of nodes to acknowledge before it's committed
Consensus algorithms compress data before writing
Only one node can process writes, creating a queue
